requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Provide safe paths of travel to, within, and through buildings and facilities, including doorways, ramps, elevators, and pedestrian pathways such as hallways and emergency exits.
requirements.evidence: Safe paths of travel to, within, and through buildings and facilities that include but are not limited to doorways, ramps, elevators, and pedestrian pathways such as hallways and emergency exits.

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: Ensure access to rooms or suites within facilities, including office areas, conference facilities, dining establishments, washrooms, and recreational areas.
requirements.evidence: Access to rooms or suites within facilities, including but not limited to office areas, conference facilities, dining establishments, washrooms and recreational areas, such as swimming pools, ice rinks, theatres, etc.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Provide safe paths of travel between facilities and public streets, pathways, sidewalks, parking areas, passenger loading and unloading zones, and bus stops.
requirements.evidence: Safe paths of travel between facilities and public streets, pathways, sidewalks, parking areas, passenger loading and unloading zones, and bus stops.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: A barrier-free path of travel must be provided from required entrances throughout all normally occupied floor areas, except for specific exempted spaces.
requirements.evidence: a barrier-free path of travel from the entrances required by Sentence 3.8.1.2.(1) and (2) shall be provided throughout all normally occupied ﬂoor areas.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Barrier-free access to a second storey or basement can be achieved by a passenger-elevating device or a compliant ramp.
requirements.evidence: Barrier-free access to a second storey or a basement can be achieved by using design alternatives such as providing a passenger-elevating device... or a ramp that complies...

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: Spaces designated for use by persons using wheelchairs within rooms or areas with fixed seats must be dispersed in each floor level, price range, and viewing section.
requirements.evidence: the number of spaces designated for use by persons using wheelchairs within rooms or areas with ﬁxed seats shall conform to Table 3.8.2.1. and be dispersed a) in each ﬂoor level of seating, c) in each price range of seating, and d) in each viewing section of seating.

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: Access to the building from the exterior and movement throughout the interior shall be by a barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: Access to the building from the exterior and movement throughout the interior of the building shall be by a barrier-free path of travel.

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: Service counters used by the general public must be accessible, including ticket counters, refreshment stands, drinking fountains, cafeteria counters, check-out counters, and bank service counters.
requirements.evidence: service counters used by the general public (e.g., ticket counters, refreshment stands, drinking fountains, cafeteria counters, check-out counters, bank service counters).

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Barrier-free path of travel must have an unobstructed width of at least 920 mm; recommended to double width for long paths.
requirements.evidence: the unobstructed width of a barrier-free path of travel shall be not less than 920 mm.

values.text: minimum 920 mm width; recommend 1840 mm for long paths

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Barrier-free path of travel may include ramps, elevators, or platform-equipped devices to overcome level changes greater than 13 mm.
requirements.evidence: A barrier-free path of travel is permitted to include ramps, passenger elevators or other platform-equipped passenger-elevating devices to overcome a dię erence in level.

values.text: ramps, elevators, or platform lifts required for >13 mm level change

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Ramps must be provided at changes in level greater than 13 mm; must be stable, firm, and slip-resistant.
requirements.evidence: be provided with sloped ﬂ oors or ramps at changes in level more than 13 mm.

values.text: ramps required for >13 mm level change

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Interior and exterior walking surfaces in barrier-free paths must be stable, firm, and slip-resistant.
requirements.evidence: be stable, ﬁ rm and slip-resistant

requirements.label: Walking Surface Openings
requirements.description: Walking surfaces in barrier-free paths must have no opening permitting passage of a sphere more than 13 mm in diameter; elongated openings must be perpendicular to travel.
requirements.evidence: have no opening that will permit the passage of a sphere more than 13 mm in diam.

requirements.label: Bevelled Changes in Level
requirements.description: Changes in level not more than 13 mm must be bevelled at a maximum slope of 1 in 2.
requirements.evidence: be bevelled at a maximum slope of 1 in 2 at changes in level not more than 13 mm

values.text: max slope 1:2 for ≤13 mm level change

requirements.label: Escalator Step Demarcation
requirements.description: Escalator steps must have yellow demarcation lines: back strip 38–50 mm wide; side lines at least 13 mm, not exceeding 50 mm.
requirements.evidence: demarcation lines on the step tread...yellow strip with a minimum of 38 mm and a maximum of 50 mm in width...side lines...at least 13 mm wide and shall not exceed 50 mm

requirements.label: Visitable Housing Features
requirements.description: Visitable homes must have no-step entrance, doorways and hallways min 900 mm wide, and wheelchair accessible bathroom with 1500 mm turning diameter and grab bars.
requirements.evidence: No-step entrance...Wider doorways (min 900 mm) and hallways (900–1200 mm)...Wheelchair accessible bathroom...min 1500 mm turning diameter.

values.text: door/hall min 900 mm; bathroom turning diameter 1500 mm

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Visual and tactile/textural warning systems should be provided on escalator steps and floor surfaces at the top and bottom of escalators for orientation.
requirements.evidence: visual and tactile/textural warning system on escalator steps and ﬂ oor surfaces at the top and bottom of the escalator

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: A barrier-free path of travel shall be provided from the entrance to exterior parking, at least one parking level, and every level served by a passenger elevator.
requirements.evidence: A barrier-free path of travel shall be provided from the entrance described in Article 3.8.1.2. to a) an exterior parking area...

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Curb ramps should be made detectable by persons whose vision is impaired or blind, or with cognitive impairments, using a different surface as an indicator or warning of a change in slope.
requirements.evidence: The curb ramps should be made detectable by persons whose vision is impaired or blind, or even persons with cognitive impairments, with a dię erent surface as an indicator or warning of a change in slope.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: If parking spaces for individuals with disabilities cannot be located close to the elevator or accessible entrance, a safe barrier-free path of travel must be designated.
requirements.evidence: If parking spaces designated for individuals with disabilities cannot be located close to the elevator or an accessible entrance, then it is necessary that a safe barrier-free path of travel is designated.

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Parking stall surfaces must be firm, slip-resistant, and level. Painting the entire area blue is unsafe as the paint is not slip-resistant under dry or wet conditions.
requirements.evidence: have a ﬁ rm, slip-resistant and level surface...the paint is not slip-resistant under dry or wet conditions.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Stall Dimensions
requirements.description: A parking stall for persons using a wheelchair or mobility aid must be 2.4 m wide, adjacent to a 2.4 m wide access aisle, with demarcation indicating no parking.
requirements.evidence: be designed as a 2.4 m wide parking stall adjacent to a 2.4 m wide access aisle where the access aisle is demarcated to indicate no parking

values.text: 2.4 m wide stall + 2.4 m wide access aisle

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Signage
requirements.description: Accessible parking stalls must be clearly marked with a vertically mounted sign (1,600–2,500 mm from finished surface) and the International Symbol of Access painted on the pavement.
requirements.evidence: clearly marked and identiﬁ ed by i) a vertically mounted sign...with the centre of the sign between 1 600 to 2 500 mm from the ﬁ nished surface, and ii) the International Symbol of Access painted on the pavement

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Height Clearance
requirements.description: Accessible parking level should allow a minimum 3,000-mm height clearance in a parkade to accommodate larger vehicles used by drivers or passengers with a disability.
requirements.evidence: The accessible parking level should allow a minimum 3 000-mm height clearance in a parkade in order to accommodate larger vehicles...

requirements.label: Passenger Loading Zone Dimensions
requirements.description: Exterior passenger loading zones must have an access aisle at least 1,500 mm wide and 6,000 mm long, a curb ramp if needed, and a clear height of at least 2,750 mm.
requirements.evidence: an access aisle not less than 1 500 mm wide and 6 000 mm long...a clear height of not less than 2 750 mm at the pull-up space

values.text: 1,500 mm x 6,000 mm aisle, 2,750 mm height

requirements.label: Wheel Stops for Sidewalk Protection
requirements.description: Wheel stops should be used to prevent vehicles from intruding onto the walk or path, maintaining a barrier-free path of travel on pedestrian walkways.
requirements.evidence: wheel stops should be used to prevent vehicles from intruding onto the walk or path.

requirements.label: Curb Ramp Placement
requirements.description: Curb ramps shall NOT be located in front of designated stall; they should be between stalls (access aisle) or to the side/adjacent to the stall.
requirements.evidence: Curb ramps shall NOT be located in front of designated stall because the vehicle will block access...In a vehicle pull-up space, the curb ramp shall be located between stalls (such as the access aisle) or to the side of or adjacent to the designated stall.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking for Adaptable Units
requirements.description: If adaptable and/or barrier-free dwelling units are provided, one parking stall per unit shall be provided meeting accessible requirements.
requirements.evidence: If adaptable and/or barrier-free dwelling units are provided, one parking stall per unit shall be provided meeting the requirements of Sentence (4).

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Stall Markings
requirements.description: Only the accessibility symbol in yellow needs to be located on the pavement of the stall to identify it as accessible; painting the entire area blue is not required.
requirements.evidence: Only the symbol in yellow needs to be located on the pavement of the stall in order to identify it as an accessible parking stall.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Directional Signage
requirements.description: Where the location of designated parking spaces is not obvious or is distant, directional signs should be placed along the route leading to them.
requirements.evidence: directional signs should be placed along the route leading to them.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Curb ramps must be at least 760 mm wide and 1,500 mm long, pointed in the direction of travel at street corners.
requirements.evidence: The curb ramp should be at least 760 mm wide and 1 500 mm long. At street corners, the curb ramp should be pointed in the direction of travel.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Tactile Walking Surface Indicators (TWSIs) must be used to alert to hazards (e.g., stairs, platform edges) and provide wayfinding for people with vision loss.
requirements.evidence: Tactile walking surface indicators (TWSIs), sometimes known as detectable warning surfaces, are standardized walking surfaces that convey information to people with vision loss through texture, and, occasionally, through sound.

requirements.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
requirements.description: TWSIs should be colour-contrasted with the surrounding walking surface; industrial yellow is preferred, or light on dark/dark on light.
requirements.evidence: TWSIs should be colour-contrasted with the surrounding walking surface. Industrial yellow is the preferred colour. However, a light colour on a dark ground surface or a dark colour on a light ground surface also works eę ectively.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Attention TWSIs must be installed at platform edges, ferry dock edges, unprotected reflecting pools, top of stairs, both sides of ground-level railway crossings, blended curbs, and moving walkways.
requirements.evidence: CNIB recommends attention TWSIs should be used at the following locations: Platform edges... The top of stairs... Both sides of ground-level railway crossings... Blended curbs...

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Attention TWSIs on platforms/ferry docks must begin 610 mm before the drop-off, run full length of unprotected edges; at stairs, commence one tread before nosing, minimum 920 mm deep.
requirements.evidence: When attention TWSIs are used on platforms and ferry docks, CNIB recommends they begin 610 millimeters before the drop-oę , running the full length of all unprotected platform/dock edges... The depth of the TWSIs used at the top of stairs should be a minimum of 920 mm.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: At railway crossings, attention TWSIs must be 1.8 m min to 4.6 m max from centerline of nearest rail; at blended curbs, set back 150–200 mm, min depth 610 mm.
requirements.evidence: At railway crossings, attention TWSIs should be located so that the edges of TWSIs are 1.8 m minimum and 4.6 m maximum from the centerline of the nearest rail... set back 150 mm to 200 mm from the curb’s edge, and they should extend a minimum depth of 610 mm in the direction of travel.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Attention TWSIs must have domes 5mm ±1mm high, top diameter 12–20mm, base 10mm ±1mm wider, min 15mm between bases, spacing per table.
requirements.evidence: Attention TWSIs should have the following speciﬁ cations: 1) The height of the ﬂ at-topped domes should be 5mm +/- 1mm. 2) The diameter of the top of the ﬂ at-topped domes should be between 12 mm and 20mm. 3) The diameter of the lower base of the ﬂ at-topped domes should be 10mm +/- 1mm more than the diameter of the top. 4) The distance between the bases of adjacent domes should be a minimum of 15mm. 5) The spacing between adjacent ﬂ at-topped domes should be adjusted depending on the size of the domes, as shown in the table below.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Guidance TWSIs must be parallel flat-topped bars, 250–550mm wide, 600mm min clearance each side, 5mm ±1mm high, top width 17–30mm, base 10mm ±1mm wider, spacing per table.
requirements.evidence: CNIB recommends guidance TWSIs have the following speciﬁ cations: They should be a minimum width of 250mm and a maximum of 550mm. They should have a minimum clearance of 600mm on either side of them. The height of the bars should be 5mm +/- 1mm. The width of the top of the ﬂ at-topped elongated bars should be between 17 mm and 30 mm. The width of the base of the bars should be 10mm +/- 1mm wider than the top.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Guidance TWSIs crossing the path of travel (e.g., bus shelters) must be at least 550mm wide.
requirements.evidence: When used to cross the path of travel, in locations such as bus shelters, guidance TWSIs should be a minimum width of 550mm to ensure detection.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Exterior walks forming part of a barrier-free path must have a cross slope ≤1:50, width ≥1,100 mm, level area at entrances, 75 mm curb at vertical drops >75 mm, contrasting texture/colour, and be free from obstructions for at least 1,980 mm height.
requirements.evidence: Exterior walks that form part of a barrier-free path of travel shall a) have a cross slope of not more than 1:50, b) be not less than 1 100 mm wide... f) be free from obstructions for the full width of the walk to not less than 1 980 mm...

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Walkways should have a hard, even, slip-resistant surface; minimum width 1,100 mm, preferred 1,500 mm; every 30 m, widen to 1,500 mm for 2,000 mm as a rest area.
requirements.evidence: An exterior walk shall be at least 1 100 mm wide... The preferred width... is 1 500 mm. It is also good practice that every 30 m, the walkway should be widened to 1 500 mm for a length of 2 000 mm to act as a rest area.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Gratings in walkways must have max clear opening 13 mm, long dimension perpendicular to travel; joints max 13 mm wide, 6 mm deep, flush with surface.
requirements.evidence: If these covers must be in walkways, the gratings shall have a maximum clear opening of 13 mm... Joints in sidewalks or between bricks or stones should not exceed 13 mm in width and 6 mm in depth, and shall be ﬂ ush with the adjoining surfaces.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Any path with slope >1:20 must be designed as a ramp; ramps must have a curb if drop-off exceeds 75 mm.
requirements.evidence: Any part of a path that has a slope steeper than 1 in 20 in a barrier-free path of travel shall be designed as a ramp. A curb on the ramp helps to prevent the front guide wheels of a wheelchair from accidentally going over the edge where the drop oę  exceeds 75 mm.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Overhead objects along walkways must be at least 1,980 mm above the walking surface to avoid hazards.
requirements.evidence: Overhead objects (e.g., signage, tree branches/limbs, free-standing staircases/escalators) should be at a height of 1 980 mm or greater to avoid becoming a hazard.

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: A level area of at least 1,500 mm by 1,500 mm must be provided in front of the entrance for ease of access.
requirements.evidence: A level area of at least 1 500 mm by 1 500 mm is to be provided in front of the entrance to allow ease of access into or out of a building.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Ramps in barrier-free paths must have clear width ≥870 mm, slope ≤1:12, level areas ≥1,500x1,500 mm at top/bottom/intermediate, handrails, and turning areas as specified.
requirements.evidence: A ramp located in a barrier-free path of travel shall a) have a clear width of not less than 870 mm... b) have a slope of not more than 1 in 12... c) have a level area not less than 1 500 by 1 500 mm at the top and bottom and at intermediate levels...

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Ramps in a barrier-free path of travel must have a minimum width of 870 mm, a maximum slope of 1 in 12, and level landings at top/bottom and every 9 m.
requirements.evidence: Ramps in a barrier-free path of travel shall have a minimum unobstructed width of 870 mm and a maximum slope of 1 in 12. A level landing of at least 1 500 mm by 1 500 mm is required at the top and bottom of the ramp. Intermediate landings shall be provided at intervals of not more than 9 m.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Where alternate routes are provided for persons with disabilities, signs are required to give directions.
requirements.evidence: Where alternate routes are provided for persons with disabilities, signs are required to give directions.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Barrier-free path of travel over 30 m must have a widened section of at least 1,500 mm for 1,500 mm at intervals not exceeding 30 m.
requirements.evidence: The width of a barrier-free path of travel that is more than 30 m long shall be increased to not less than 1 500 mm for a length of 1 500 mm at intervals not exceeding 30 m.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Maximum ramp slopes vary by occupancy: 1:10 for assembly/care/residential, 1:6 for mercantile/industrial, 1:8 for other, 1:10 for exterior ramps.
requirements.evidence: the maximum slope of a ramp shall be a) 1 in 10 in any assembly, care, treatment, detention or residential occupancy, b) 1 in 6 in rooms or ﬂ oor areas classiﬁ ed as a mercantile or industrial occupancy, c) 1 in 8 in any other ﬂ oor area, and d) 1 in 10 for an exterior ramp.

requirements.label: Ramp Edge Protection
requirements.description: Ramps must have provisions such as curbs, handrails, or guards to prevent wheelchairs or mobility aids from going over the edge.
requirements.evidence: A variety of provisions can be used to prevent people using wheelchairs or other mobility aids from accidentally going over the edge of a ramp. Curbs are often combined with handrails and guards.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Floors or walks in a barrier-free path of travel with a slope steeper than 1 in 20 must be designed as ramps.
requirements.evidence: Floors or walks in a barrier-free path of travel having a slope steeper than 1 in 20 shall be designed as ramps.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Wayfinding design must provide logical/intuitive space, textural contrasts, tactile cues, acoustics, color/brightness contrast, and signage (tactile, Braille, audible).
requirements.evidence: Wayﬁ nding design organizes the built environment to provide useful information for navigation... Logical and intuitive space... Textural contrasts and tactile cues... Signage, including tactile, Braille, and audible signs...

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: At least 50% of pedestrian entrances, including the primary entrance, must be barrier-free, with accessible walkways from public thoroughfares and parking.
requirements.evidence: not less than 50% of the pedestrian entrances, including the primary entrance... shall be barrier-free.

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: The primary entrance and at least 50% of total entrances must be barrier-free, providing alternate egress routes in emergencies.
requirements.evidence: The primary entrance of the building shall be designed to be barrier-free. The remaining 50% (at least) of the total number of entrances in the building shall also be barrier-free. This provides alternate egress routes in case of an emergency.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Clear and sufficient signage is required to indicate the way to and location of all doors designated for use by people with physical disabilities or mobility issues.
requirements.evidence: Clear and suĚ  cient signage is required to indicate the way to and location of all doors designated for use by people with physical disabilities or mobility issues.

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: Barrier-free entrances must comply with Article 3.8.3.3. If a walkway or bridge connects two barrier-free storeys, the path must be barrier-free.
requirements.evidence: If a walkway or pedestrian bridge connects two barrier-free storeys in dię erent buildings, the path of travel from one storey to the other storey by means of the walkway or bridge shall be barrier-free.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: If an entrance is equipped with a security system, both visual and audible signals must indicate when the door lock is released.
requirements.evidence: If an entrance is equipped with a security system, both visual and audible signals shall be used to indicate when the door lock is released.

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: All entrances to facilities only accessible from the outside (e.g., strip shopping centres) must be barrier-free.
requirements.evidence: The entrances to all these facilities shall be barrier-free in order to minimize or eliminate obstacles that would require assistance to accessing necessary services and social or recreational opportunities.

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Every doorway in a barrier-free path of travel must have a clear opening width of at least 850 mm when the door is fully open.
requirements.evidence: Every doorway that is located in a barrier-free path of travel shall have a clear width not less than 850 mm when the door is in the open position.

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Every doorway into rooms within a suite of residential occupancy must have a clear width of at least 850 mm when the door is open.
requirements.evidence: Every doorway into rooms within a suite of residential occupancy shall have a clear width not less than 850 mm when the door is in the open position.

requirements.label: Accessible Door Hardware
requirements.description: Door operating devices must not require tight grasping or twisting of the wrist; lever handles are preferred.
requirements.evidence: Door operating devices shall be of a design which does not require tight grasping and twisting of the wrist as the only means of operation.

requirements.label: Door Hardware Height
requirements.description: Door hardware should be installed between 900 mm and 1,065 mm above the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: Door hardware should be installed between 900 mm and 1 065 mm above the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor.

requirements.label: Door Latch Side Clearance
requirements.description: Doorways must provide at least 600 mm of clear space on the latch side for wheelchair or mobility aid users.
requirements.evidence: the doorway shall provide a minimum of 600 mm of clear space on the latch side of the door to allow a person in a wheelchair, or those using other mobility aids, ease of movement and safety.

requirements.label: Automatic Door Operation
requirements.description: Power-assist doors must be used if required force or clear space is not achievable for accessibility.
requirements.evidence: Power-assist doors shall be used if either or both the required force and a clear space is not achievable.

requirements.label: Threshold Height and Bevel
requirements.description: A threshold for a doorway must not be more than 13 mm higher than the finished floor and must be bevelled for wheelchair passage.
requirements.evidence: A threshold for a doorway... shall be not more than 13 mm higher than the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor surface and shall be bevelled to facilitate the passage of wheelchairs.

requirements.label: Automatic Door Operation
requirements.description: Every door in a barrier-free path of travel through certain entrances must be equipped with a power door operator, with exceptions for small suites.
requirements.evidence: every door that provides a barrier-free path of travel through an entrance referred to in Article 3.8.1.2... shall be equipped with a power door operator that allows persons to activate the opening of the door from either side if the entrance serves a) a hotel, b) a building of Group B, Division 2 major occupancy, or c) a building of Group A, Group B, Division 3, Group D or E major occupancy more than 500 m²  in building area.

values.applicable_occupancies: hotel, Group B Div 2, Group A/B3/D/E >500m²

requirements.label: Threshold Visual Contrast
requirements.description: Thresholds should be identified by an alternate colour or brightness to indicate a change in level.
requirements.evidence: the threshold should be identiﬁ ed by an alternate colour or brightness to indicate a change in level from area to area.

requirements.label: Door Opening Force
requirements.description: A closer for a door in a barrier-free path of travel must permit opening with a force not more than 38 N for exterior doors.
requirements.evidence: a closer for a door in a barrier-free path of travel shall be designed to permit the door to open when the force applied... is not more than a) 38 N in the case of an exterior door

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Doors in a barrier-free path of travel must have clear space on the latch side: 600 mm if door swings toward approach, 300 mm if away.
requirements.evidence: a door in a barrier-free path of travel shall have a clear space on the latch side... not less than a) 600 mm... b) 300 mm...

requirements.label: Automatic Door Operation
requirements.description: Power-operated doors are required at entrances of assembly buildings, care facilities, or buildings ≥500 m2. Must be manually operable during power failure.
requirements.evidence: A power-operated door is required to be installed at entrances... in a care facility... or in any building of major occupancy 500 m2 or greater.

values.text: ≥500 m2, manual operation in power failure

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: Push button operators for power-assisted doors must be mounted 900–1,100 mm high for ease of access and use.
requirements.evidence: The push button operators for the power-assisted door should be mounted 900 mm - 1 100 mm high for ease of access and use.

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Surfaces of ramps, landings, and treads must be slip resistant; public areas require colour contrast or distinctive pattern at leading edges.
requirements.evidence: shall have a ﬁ nish that is slip resistant, and... have either a colour contrast or a distinctive pattern to demarcate the leading edge

values.text: Slip resistant; colour contrast or pattern

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Where stairs are used to define spaces, barrier-free ramps or equivalent accessible spaces must be provided for wheelchair users.
requirements.evidence: shall provide properly designed barrier-free ramps to these areas or provide equally interesting spaces on the entrance level for those using wheelchairs.

requirements.label: Area Rug Secured
requirements.description: Obstructions within 1,980 mm of the floor must not project more than 100 mm into corridors unless clearance below is less than 680 mm.
requirements.evidence: obstructions located within 1 980 mm of the ﬂ oor shall not project more than 100 mm horizontally into an exit passageway...

values.text: ≤100 mm projection unless <680 mm clearance below

requirements.label: Glazing Visual Indication
requirements.description: Entrances must be identified by contrasting colours for door frames, handles, awnings, etc. Entrances and vestibules should be well lit.
requirements.evidence: Entrances shall be identiﬁ ed by the use of contrasting colours for door frames, handles, awnings, etc. Entrances and vestibules should be well lit.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Minimum width of public corridors and unobstructed corridors is 1,100 mm. Dead-end corridors not more than 3 m long unless exceptions apply.
requirements.evidence: The minimum width of a public corridor shall be 1 100 mm... a dead-end corridor is permitted provided it is not more than 3 m long.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Slip resistance and colour contrast or pattern required at the beginning and end of ramps. Treads and landings must be slip resistant.
requirements.evidence: shall have a ﬁ nish that is slip resistant, and... have either a colour contrast or a distinctive pattern... as well as the beginning and the end of a ramp.

values.text: Slip resistant; colour contrast or pattern

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: Vestibules in barrier-free paths must allow wheelchair movement and provide at least 1,200 mm between doors plus width of any door swinging into the space.
requirements.evidence: shall provide a distance between 2 doors in series of not less than 1 200 mm plus the width of any door that swings into the space

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Nosings on stairs should be of a contrasting colour or colour tone from the respective risers and treads, and should be distinguishable whether approaching the stairs from the top or bottom.
requirements.evidence: Nosings on stairs should be of a contrasting colour or colour tone from the respective risers and treads, and should be distinguishable whether approaching the stairs from the top or bottom.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: A ramp shall have handrails on both sides.
requirements.evidence: A ramp shall have handrails on both sides.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes

requirements.description: Light switches should be mounted between 1200 mm and 1400 mm from the finished floor (measured to the centre line of the plate).
requirements.evidence: Light switches should be mounted between 1 200 mm – 1 400 mm when measured from the centre line of the plate to the finished floor.

requirements.label: Control Height (Thermostats, etc.)
requirements.description: Controls such as thermostats should be mounted at 1400 mm from the finished floor (measured to the centre line of the plate).
requirements.evidence: Controls, like thermostats should be mounted at 1 400 mm when measured from the centre line of the plate to the finished floor.

requirements.label: Colour Contrast for Doors
requirements.description: Provide colour contrast between the door trim, door, and wall for better visibility.
requirements.evidence: Colour contrast between the door trim, door and wall.

requirements.label: Tactile Differentiation for Wayfinding
requirements.description: Effective tactile differentiation between living areas should be provided for wayfinding and safety.
requirements.evidence: Effective tactile-differentiation between living areas for wayfinding and safety.

requirements.label: Lever-Handled Door Hardware
requirements.description: Lever-handled hardware should be used on all doors that latch.
requirements.evidence: Lever-handled hardware on all doors that latch.

requirements.label: Minimum Hallway Width
requirements.description: A minimum width of 1200 mm is needed for hallways to allow movement of a person who uses a wheelchair.
requirements.evidence: A minimum width of 1 200 mm is needed for movement of a person who uses a wheelchair.

requirements.label: Window Sill Height (Living/Dining)
requirements.description: Window sills in living and dining rooms should be no higher than 600 mm from the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: window sill in living and dining rooms no higher than 600 mm.

requirements.label: Window Sill Height (Bedroom/Bathroom/Other)
requirements.description: Window sills in bedrooms, bathrooms, and other rooms should be no higher than 750 mm from the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: window sills should be no higher than 750 mm when measured from the finished floor.

requirements.label: Window Control Height
requirements.description: Window controls should be placed 750 mm to 1000 mm from the floor.
requirements.evidence: window controls should be placed in an area 750 mm to 1 000 mm when measured from the floor.

requirements.label: Bariatric Accommodation (Doorways and Hallways)
requirements.description: For bariatric accommodation, doorways should have a clear width opening of 1100 mm - 1200 mm, and hallways should be 1200 mm – 1500 mm in width.
requirements.evidence: doorways...should have a clear width opening of 1100 mm - 1200 mm, and hallways should be 1200 mm – 1500 mm in width

requirements.label: Kitchen Counter Heights and Knee Space
requirements.description: Counter tops should have at least two heights (915 mm and 760 mm) with knee space beneath. Height-adjustable cooktop and sink recommended.
requirements.evidence: Counter tops with at least two counter heights (915 mm and 760 mm) and with knee space beneath. Height-adjustable cook top...height-adjustable sink

requirements.label: Bathroom Transfer Space
requirements.description: A transfer space of 900 - 1000 mm located beside the toilet is required for wheelchair users.
requirements.evidence: A transfer space (for those who use wheelchairs) of 900 - 1 000 mm located beside the toilet.

requirements.label: Shower Size and Lip
requirements.description: Showers should be 1500 x 1500 mm with no lip for barrier-free access.
requirements.evidence: 1 500 x 1 500 mm shower with no lip

requirements.label: Grab Bars in Bathrooms
requirements.description: Grab bars should be provided for the toilet, shower, and bathtub.
requirements.evidence: Grab bars for the toilet, shower and bathtub.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: A hotel or motel suite must have a barrier-free path of travel to its entry, including wider doorways into the suite and bathrooms within.
requirements.evidence: A hotel or motel suite must have a barrier-free path of travel to its entry. This includes wider doorways into the suite and wider doorways to the bathrooms within.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: A barrier-free path of travel may have a ramp leading to ground level as an alternative to an exterior exit at ground level.
requirements.evidence: have an exterior exit at ground level, or have a ramp leading to ground level.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Every floor area above or below the first storey that is not sprinklered and has a barrier-free path of travel shall be served by an elevator.
requirements.evidence: Every ﬂ oor area above or below the ﬁ rst storey that is not sprinklered throughout and that has a barrier-free path of travel shall be served by an elevator

requirements.label: Tactile Warning Strips at Elevation Changes
requirements.description: A downward change in elevation in a barrier-free path of travel shall be signalled by a 600 mm wide tactile warning strip placed 250 mm from the edge and for the full width, identified using colour and brightness contrast.
requirements.evidence: a downward change in elevation shall be signalled by the use of a 600 mm wide tactile warning strip placed 250 mm from the edge and for the full width of a stair, escalator, moving walkway, ramp or platform, and identiﬁ ed using colour and brightness contrast.

requirements.label: Balcony Refuge Dimensions
requirements.description: A balcony required as a refuge must have direct barrier-free access, be at least 1.5 m deep, and provide at least 1.5 m² per nonambulatory occupant and 0.5 m² per ambulatory occupant.
requirements.evidence: be not less than 1.5 m deep from the outside face of the exterior wall to the inside edge of the balcony, and provide not less than 1.5 m² of balcony space for each nonambulatory occupant and 0.5 m² for each ambulatory occupant.

requirements.label: Grab Bars in Bathtubs
requirements.description: Grab bars must be installed in every bathing unit, whether designated accessible or not, to provide safety and accommodate guests needing moderate assistance.
requirements.evidence: Grab bars must be installed in every bathing unit, whether designated accessible or not.

requirements.label: Bathtub Clear Floor Space and Grab Bar Placement
requirements.description: Bathtubs must have a clear floor space at least 900 mm wide along its length, and grab bars 1200 mm long located vertically and horizontally at specified heights above the rim.
requirements.evidence: have a clear ﬂ oor space at least 900 mm wide along its length, ... grab bars that ... are 1 200 mm long located vertically at the end ... with the lower end between 180 mm and 280 mm above the bathtub rim, and ... are 1 200 mm long located horizontally along the length ... at 180 mm to 280 mm above the bathtub rim

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: An audible signal shall be accompanied with a visual signal to ensure that people who are deaf or hard of hearing and people who are blind receive notification of the alert and/or alarm in an emergency.
requirements.evidence: An audible signal shall be accompanied with a visual signal to ensure that people who are deaf or hard of hearing and people who are blind receive notiﬁ cation of the alert and/or alarm in an emergency.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Alert signals must be clearly audible in continuously staffed locations and throughout floor areas, and must be accompanied by visual cues in all areas.
requirements.evidence: alert signals are clearly audible in continuously staę ed locations, and ... throughout the ﬂ oor area. ... All audible sounds must be accompanied with visual cues

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Tactile warning strips in contrasting colours must be used at the top of escalators, ramps, moving walkways, stairways, and platform edges not served by handrails or guards.
requirements.evidence: tactile warning strips in contrasting colours shall be used at the top of escalators, ramps, moving walkways, stairways, and at the edge of platforms not served by handrails or guards

requirements.label: Fire Alarm Sound Pressure Levels
requirements.description: Fire alarm signal sound pressure level must not exceed 110 dBA in any normally occupied area; in sleeping rooms, not less than 75 dBA with doors closed.
requirements.evidence: The ﬁ re alarm signal sound pressure level shall be not more than 110 dBA ... The sound pressure level in a sleeping room ... shall be not less than 75 dBA ... doors ... closed.

requirements.label: Alarm Sound Level Above Ambient
requirements.description: Fire alarm audible signal devices must be at least 10 dBA above ambient noise, and not less than 65 dBA.
requirements.evidence: sound pressure level from a ﬁ re alarm system’s audible signal device ... shall be not less than 10 dBA above the ambient noise level without being less than 65 dBA.

requirements.label: Manual Silencing of Audible Signal Devices
requirements.description: Audible signal devices in dwelling units must include a means for manual silencing for up to 10 minutes, after which they restore to normal operation.
requirements.evidence: audible signal devices located within a dwelling unit shall include a means for them to be manually silenced for a period of not more than 10 min, after which time the devices shall restore themselves to normal operation.

requirements.label: Visual Signal Device Installation
requirements.description: Visual signal devices must be installed in addition to audible devices in buildings with fire alarm systems, conforming to CAN/ULC-S526, and be visible within suites.
requirements.evidence: Visual signal devices shall be installed in addition to audible signal devices in buildings required to have a ﬁ re alarm system and shall conform to CAN/ULC-S526 ... so that the signal from at least one device is visible within a suite

requirements.label: Vibration Devices for Deaf/Hard of Hearing
requirements.description: Suites for persons who are deaf, hard of hearing, or deaf-blind should be equipped with visual signalling devices and a vibration device for the bed.
requirements.evidence: Suites designated for use by persons who are deaf, hard of hearing or deaf-blind should also be equipped with visual signalling devices and a vibration device for the bed

requirements.label: Tactile Warning Strip Placement and Contrast
requirements.description: Tactile warning strips must begin 850 mm back from the edge, be full width, feel/sound different, and be in contrasting colour to surrounding flooring.
requirements.evidence: The required tactile warning strip shall begin 850 mm back from the edge in question and for its full width. It should feel and/or sound noticeably dię erent ... and be in a contrasting colour

requirements.label: Downward Elevation Change Warning
requirements.description: A 600 mm wide tactile warning strip, placed 250 mm from the edge, must signal a downward change in elevation in a barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: a downward change in elevation shall be signalled by the use of a 600 mm wide tactile warning strip placed 250 mm from the edge and for the full width

requirements.label: Pattern and Colour Blocking for Key Access
requirements.description: Tactile warning strips must be in contrasting colours to assist persons with limited vision and those who are blind.
requirements.evidence: tactile warning strips in contrasting colours shall be used ... To assist persons with limited vision and those who are blind

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: Built-in shelves or counters for public telephones must be level, not less than 265 mm deep, and provide a clear adjacent space not less than 265 mm wide and 265 mm high.
requirements.evidence: Shelves or counters...shall be level and...not be less than 265 mm deep, have...a clear space adjacent...not less than 265 mm wide, having no obstruction within 265 mm above the surface.

values.text: 265 mm deep, 265 mm wide, 265 mm clearance above

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Coin slots and other features of public telephones must be within reach of a person sitting in a wheelchair and not higher than 1 370 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: Coin slots and other features shall be within reach of a person sitting in a wheelchair. These should not be higher than 1 370 mm above the ﬂ oor.

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: The top surface of a section of the shelf or counter serving at least one telephone shall be not more than 865 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: The top surface of a section of the shelf or counter...shall be not more than 865 mm above the ﬂ oor.

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: If a wall-hung telephone is provided above a shelf or counter, the receiver and coin slot must not be more than 1 370 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: If a wall-hung telephone is provided...it shall be located so that the receiver and coin slot are not more than 1 370 mm above the ﬂ oor.

requirements.label: Cane-Detectable Partition at Telephones
requirements.description: Public telephones or banks of telephones should retain a cane-detectable partition for people with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: should retain a cane-detectable partition in order for a person with visual disabilities to detect that s/he is approaching an area with projecting shelves and telephones.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: If an entrance is equipped with a security system, both visual and audible signals must be used to indicate when the door lock is released.
requirements.evidence: If an entrance is equipped with a security system, both visual and audible signals shall be used to indicate when the door lock is released.

requirements.label: TTY/TDD Public Telephone Availability
requirements.description: At least one telephone with a built-in telecommunication device for the deaf (TTY/TDD) shall be provided if public telephones are installed.
requirements.evidence: At least one telephone with a built-in telecommunication device for the deaf (TTY/TDD) shall be provided if public telephones are installed.

requirements.label: TTY Signage in Large Complexes
requirements.description: In large complexes with more than one bank of pay telephones, signs should indicate the location of the TTY.
requirements.evidence: where more than one bank of pay telephones is provided, signs should indicate the location of the TTY.

requirements.label: Assistive Listening System in Assembly Areas
requirements.description: All assembly areas over 100 m² must be equipped with an assistive listening system encompassing the entire seating area, except induction loop systems may cover half.
requirements.evidence: all assembly areas with an area of more than 100 m² shall be equipped with an assistive listening system encompassing the entire seating area.

requirements.label: Assistive Listening Devices for 50+ Occupancy
requirements.description: Assistive Listening Devices (ALD) should be installed in all areas of any building where occupancy might be 50 persons and more.
requirements.evidence: Assistive Listening Devices (ALD) should be installed in all areas of any building where occupancy might be 50 persons and more.

requirements.label: Annual ALD System Check
requirements.description: All Assisted Listening Systems should be checked once per year to ensure proper operation.
requirements.evidence: All Assisted Listening Systems should be checked once per year to ensure proper operation.

requirements.label: Induction Loop Receiver Provision
requirements.description: Where an induction loop system is deployed, the building should have 1 loop receiver for every 50 occupants.
requirements.evidence: the building should have 1 loop receiver for every 50 occupants

requirements.label: Induction Loop System Certification
requirements.description: Installation of induction loop shall comply with IEC60118-4 2014 standard, and a Certificate of Conformity should be issued; checked annually.
requirements.evidence: Installation of induction loop shall comply with the IEC60118-4 2014 standard, and a Certiﬁ cate of Conformity should be issued...Once per year, each loop installation should be checked and a Certiﬁ cate of Conformity shall be issued.

requirements.label: Magnetic Counter Loop in Health Care
requirements.description: Health care locations manned by staff should be equipped with a magnetic counter loop system and the 't coil' symbol should be displayed.
requirements.evidence: Heath Care locations manned by staę  should be equipped with a magnetic counter loop system and the “t coil” symbol should be displayed.

requirements.label: Braille, Visual, and Audio Cues
requirements.description: Accessibility signs for universal toilet rooms must include Braille identification by use of Braille dots not less than 1 mm in relief, located directly below the tactile signage.
requirements.evidence: include Braille identiﬁ cation by use of Braille dots not less than 1 mm in relief, located directly below the tactile signage.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Signs incorporating the international symbol of accessibility for persons with physical disabilities shall be installed to indicate the location of a barrier-free entrance.
requirements.evidence: Signs incorporating the international symbol of accessibility for persons with physical disabilities shall be installed to indicate the location of a barrier-free entrance.

requirements.label: Non-Text Diagrams and Symbols
requirements.description: Facilities and services for persons with a specific disability shall be identified using nationally recognized symbols.
requirements.evidence: Facilities and services for persons with a speciﬁ c disability shall be identiﬁ ed using nationally recognized symbols.

requirements.label: Tactile Signage Requirements
requirements.description: Tactile signage must be not less than 60 mm high, raised ~0.7 mm, located ≤1200 mm above floor, ≤150 mm from entrance, and contrasting in colour.
requirements.evidence: be not less than 60 mm high, raised approximately 0.7 mm above the surface, be located not more than 1 200 mm above the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor, begin not more than 150 mm from the door or entrance, be contrasting in colour

values.text: 60 mm high, 0.7 mm raised, ≤1200 mm height, ≤150 mm from entrance

requirements.label: Non-Text Diagrams and Symbols
requirements.description: Use graphics or internationally recognized symbols to indicate direction or identify facilities and services for people with language or cognitive difficulties.
requirements.evidence: The use of graphics or internationally recognized symbols to indicate direction or identify facilities and services will be beneficial to persons with...little or no knowledge of the local written language.

requirements.label: Braille, Visual, and Audio Cues
requirements.description: Provide tactile signs with raised letters, and consider audio cues (e.g., Talking Signs) for wayfinding for people with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: speciﬁ c tactile signs with letters not less than 60 mm high, raised 0.7 mm above the surface...Recent technology (such as a product known as Talking Signs)...provides beams of invisible light to receivers...

values.text: Letters not less than 60 mm high, raised 0.7 mm above surface

requirements.label: Pattern and Colour Blocking for Key Access
requirements.description: Use colour/contrast cues, patterns, and textures to define locations of doorways, controls, and changes in direction for orientation.
requirements.evidence: colour/contrast cues, i.e., used to deﬁ ne the location of doorways, building controls...patterns...textures (dię erent ﬂ ooring materials to indicate change in direction or use)

requirements.label: Haptic and Tactile Maps
requirements.description: Provide tactile maps and directories with raised characters for orientation for people who are blind.
requirements.evidence: Building directories should be conveniently located, tactile in nature...Characters that are raised at least 0.7 mm are needed.

requirements.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
requirements.description: Signs and floor identification for orientation should offer maximum colour contrast, preferably white on black or black on white, and be glare-free.
requirements.evidence: it is recommended that white on black or black on white is used, as this combination produces the best legibility...sign surfaces be processed to prevent glare.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Use tactile cues (different flooring), colour contrast, and changes in illumination to warn of changes in direction or use.
requirements.evidence: tactile cues, i.e., dię erent types of ﬂ ooring that mark a pathway, or change from one area to another, thus serving as a warning that a change in direction is to take place

requirements.label: Multilingual Signage
requirements.description: Where wording is used on signs, language should be simple and concise to be accessible for most building users.
requirements.evidence: if wording is to be used on signs, the language on them should be simple and concise to be accessible for most building users.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Temporary pedestrian routes around construction sites must be accessible, clear of obstructions, and replicate positive characteristics of existing walks.
requirements.evidence: All pedestrians shall be provided with safe passage through or around any construction or demolition site...temporary walking surface that replicates to the greatest extent practical the positive characteristics of the existing walk being closed.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Where sidewalks are obstructed, provide temporary sidewalks with ramps and handrails as needed, ensuring accessibility for all users.
requirements.evidence: Where construction operations necessitate the obstruction of a sidewalk, a temporary sidewalk shall be provided...Handrails and clearly demarcated temporary ramps with detectable warnings may be needed.

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Temporary walking surfaces must be slip-resistant even when wet; address mud, snow, and ice promptly.
requirements.evidence: Ensure that temporary walking surfaces such as plywood are selected for slip-resistance even when wet. Mud, snow, and ice accumulations require timely attention.

requirements.label: Covered Way Minimum Dimensions and Features
requirements.description: Covered ways at construction sites must have a clear height ≥2.5m, clear width ≥1.5m, weathertight roof, smooth enclosure, adequate lighting, and lockable gates.
requirements.evidence: A covered way shall a) have a clear height of not less than 2.5 m, b) have a clear width of not less than 1.5 m...g) be adequately lighted...h) have...a gate not less than 1 200 mm high...

requirements.label: Obstruction-Free Temporary Routes
requirements.description: Temporary sidewalks and adjacent sidewalks must be kept clear of obstructions at all times; no materials, scrap, or equipment on walking surfaces.
requirements.evidence: where a sidewalk exists adjacent to the site, it shall be kept clear of obstructions at all times...Materials, scrap, and equipment cannot be placed on the walking surface as this poses a hazard for all users.

requirements.label: Cane-Detectable Zone
requirements.description: Fences and covered ways must not have objects protruding beyond the cane-detectable zone to ensure safety for people with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Care is called for in erecting fences and covered ways so that no objects protrude beyond the cane-detectable zone.

requirements.label: Advance Notice and Intelligible Signage
requirements.description: Provide intelligible signage with symbols, arrows, and large print in contrasting colours to give advance notice of closures, detours, or delays.
requirements.evidence: Use symbols, arrows, and large-sized print text in contrasting colours identified with safety and danger...Advance notice—time and positional—results in more-informed decisions by all users...

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Outdoor public use eating areas must be located on an accessible path of travel or trail.
requirements.evidence: locate on an accessible path of travel or trail;

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: At least 20% of tables and no fewer than one in outdoor public use eating areas must be accessible.
requirements.evidence: ensure a minimum of twenty percent (20%) of tables and no fewer than one (1) in outdoor public use eating area are accessible;

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: Accessible tables must provide suitable knee and toe clearances and a clear space of 2000 mm minimum on all sides.
requirements.evidence: ensure accessible tables provide suitable knee and toe clearances (Figure 105b); provide a clear space of 2000 mm (minimum) on all sides of the table (Figure 105a);

values.text: 2000 mm clear space on all sides

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Ground surface leading to and under tables must be firm, stable, and no steeper than 1:50 (2%).
requirements.evidence: ensure ground surface leading to and under tables is ﬁ rm, stable, and no steeper than 1:50 (2%);

requirements.label: Glazing Visual Indication
requirements.description: Barbecues in outdoor public-use eating areas must be placed away from accessible paths and on a surface with high tonal and textural contrast.
requirements.evidence: ensure that they are placed away from the accessible path of travel and on a surface with high tonal and textural contrast with the adjacent surfaces;

requirements.label: Accessible Washroom in Outdoor Areas
requirements.description: Where washrooms are provided in outdoor public use eating areas, at least one universal toilet room per cluster of regular washrooms must be accessible.
requirements.evidence: ensure accessible features (e.g., at least one universal toilet room per cluster of regular washrooms).

requirements.label: Accessible Trailhead Signage
requirements.description: Designated trailheads must include information signage at key entrance, exit, and decision points.
requirements.evidence: ensure that designated trailheads with information signage are integrated as part of the trail design at key entrance and exit points along the trail, intermediate areas on lengthy trails, or decision points

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Trail entrance/exit points must provide 850 mm to 1000 mm clear opening and be free of obstructions.
requirements.evidence: provide 850 mm to 1000 mm clear opening whether entrance includes a gate, bollard, or other entrance design; and ensure that entrances are maintained and clear of obstructions

requirements.label: Trail Clear Width and Passing Spaces
requirements.description: Trails must have a clear width of 1000 mm minimum (1800 mm preferred); if less than 1800 mm, provide 1800 mm x 1800 mm passing spaces every 30 m.
requirements.evidence: provide clear width of 1000 mm (minimum) to 1800 mm (preferred); where the clear width is less than 1800 mm, provide a passing space of 1800 mm wide by 1800 mm (minimum) long, at intervals no more than 30 m

values.text: 1000 mm min width, 1800 mm preferred; passing space 1800x1800 mm every 30 m

requirements.label: Trail Headroom Clearance
requirements.description: Ensure headroom clearance is 2100 mm minimum above the trail and no obstructions or projections along trail.
requirements.evidence: ensure headroom clearance is 2100 mm (minimum) above the trail; and ensure that there are no obstructions or projections along trail.

requirements.label: Trail Surface Openings
requirements.description: Trail surface openings must not allow passage of objects >20 mm diameter (13 mm preferred); elongated openings oriented perpendicular to travel.
requirements.evidence: openings must not allow passage of an object that has a diameter of more than 20 mm (13 mm diameter preferred), and that any elongated openings are oriented approximately perpendicular to the direction of travel;

requirements.label: Trail Surface Stability and Durability
requirements.description: Trail surfaces must be firm, stable, resistant to weather, and able to sustain typical wear and tear between maintenance cycles.
requirements.evidence: ensure that surface is ﬁ rm and stable; ensure that surface is resistant to damage by normal weather conditions, with ability to sustain typical wear and tear between planned maintenance cycles;

requirements.label: Trail Surface Information Signage
requirements.description: Type of surface and expected changing conditions must be identified in information signage at trailhead.
requirements.evidence: ensure that type of surface used and expected conditions that may change over time are identiﬁ ed in information signage provided at trailhead.

requirements.label: Trail Slope Requirements
requirements.description: Provide running and cross slopes as gentle as possible, as permitted by terrain, to minimize user effort and provide even surfaces.
requirements.evidence: provide a running slope that is as gentle as possible, as permitted by the terrain, to minimize amount of strength and stamina required to use the trail; and ensure that cross slopes are as gentle as possible, as permitted by the terrain, to provide an even surface for diverse users

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Where ramps are provided on trails, running slope must not exceed 1:10 (10%) and comply with ramp requirements from Section 2.2.
requirements.evidence: provide a running slope no greater than 1:10 (10%); and with the exception of the running slope, ensure compliance with ramp requirements from Section 2.2

requirements.label: Edge Protection for Trails Adjacent to Hazards
requirements.description: Where trails are adjacent to water or drop-offs, provide edge protection: elevated barrier, top at least 50 mm above trail, not impeding drainage.
requirements.evidence: provide edge protection with the following requirements: constitute of an elevated barrier ... top of the edge protection at 50 mm (minimum) high above the trail surface; ... not to impede the drainage

requirements.label: Trailhead Signage Content and Contrast
requirements.description: Trailhead signage must include trail length, surface type, width, slopes, features, and have high tonal contrast with sans serif font.
requirements.evidence: provide signage with the following information ... ensure that signage text has high tonal contrast with its background in order to assist with visual recognition, with text that includes characters that use a sans serif font.

requirements.label: Accessible Trail Information in Other Media
requirements.description: Where other media (websites, brochures) provide trail information, include the same information as trailhead signage.
requirements.evidence: where other media such as park websites or brochures are used to provide information about the recreational trail ... provide the same information identiﬁ ed on the trailhead signage.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Provide directional signage at strategic locations to identify accessible tables and public use eating areas.
requirements.evidence: provide directional signage at strategic locations to identify the location(s) of accessible tables and or public use eating areas;

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Provide a clear opening of 1000 mm at entrances, including gates or bollards, and ensure accessible routes to and within outdoor spaces.
requirements.evidence: provide 1000 mm clear opening whether entrance includes a gate, bollard. or other entrance design.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Provide clear width of 1000 mm (minimum) and headroom clearance of 2100 mm (minimum) above beach access route.
requirements.evidence: provide clear width of 1000 mm (minimum); and provide headroom clearance of 2100 mm (minimum) above beach access route.

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Ensure surface is firm and stable; constructed surfaces must have bevels and slopes within specified limits.
requirements.evidence: ensure surface is ﬁ rm and stable;

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Provide a ramp where changes in level are greater than 200 mm; running slope no greater than 1:10 (10%).
requirements.evidence: provide a ramp where changes in level are greater than 200 mm.

values.text: ramp for >200 mm level change, 1:10 max slope

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Boardwalks must have a clear width of 1000 mm (minimum); passing space of 1800 mm x 1800 mm at intervals no more than 30 m if width <1800 mm.
requirements.evidence: provide clear width of 1000 mm (minimum); ... passing space of 1800 mm wide by 1800 mm (minimum) long, at intervals no more than 30 m;

values.text: 1000 mm width, 1800 mm passing space every 30 m

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Boardwalk surfaces must be firm and stable; openings must not allow passage of an object >20 mm (13 mm preferred), oriented perpendicular to travel.
requirements.evidence: ensure surface is ﬁ rm and stable; ... openings must not allow passage of an object that has a diameter of more than 20 mm (13 mm diameter preferred)

values.text: firm/stable, max 20 mm opening (13 mm preferred)

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Boardwalks must have headroom clearance of 2100 mm (minimum) above the boardwalk.
requirements.evidence: ensure that headroom clearance is 2100 mm (minimum) above the boardwalk.

requirements.label: Edge Protection on Boardwalks
requirements.description: Provide edge protection that is 50 mm (minimum) high on boardwalks; design must allow suitable drainage.
requirements.evidence: provide edge protection that is 50 mm (minimum) high; and ... design allows suitable drainage

requirements.label: Accessible Trailhead Amenities
requirements.description: Exterior amenities (e.g., parking, accessible routes, public washrooms) should be provided at trailheads and reviewed for accessibility compliance.
requirements.evidence: Are EXTERIOR AMENITIES provided at trailheads (e.g., parking, accessible routes, public washrooms, etc.)?

requirements.label: Trail Clear Width and Obstruction-Free
requirements.description: Trail clear width must be at least 1000 mm (1800 mm preferred); vegetation and features must not obstruct accessible route.
requirements.evidence: Is the CLEAR WIDTH of the trail at least 1000 mm (1800 mm preferred)? ... does not create obstruction or projection along accessible route.

requirements.label: Trail Edge Protection and Marking

requirements.label: Trail Headroom Clearance

requirements.label: Trail Slope

requirements.evidence: Is the RUNNING SLOPE as gentle as possible, as permitted by the terrain?

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring

requirements.evidence: Is the TRAIL SURFACE ﬁ rm and stable? Identify type of surface and material used to meet accessibility requirements.

requirements.description: Provide trail name/identification signage at trailheads and key access points, with accessibility features (e.g., large print, strong tonal contrast, pictograms).

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Signage should include trail name, map, length, surface type, running slope, cross slope, and manager/operator in accessible format.
requirements.evidence: Identifying this information in accessible format allows users of all ages and abilities to make an informed decision about using the trail.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Barriers to accessibility (e.g., steep slopes, difficult topography) should be identified on signage at strategic locations.

requirements.label: Inclusive Play Space Consultation
requirements.description: Consultation with the public, persons with disabilities, and Accessibility Advisory Committee is required when constructing or redeveloping outdoor play spaces.
requirements.evidence: consultation on the needs of children and caregivers with various disabilities must occur with: a) the public and persons with disabilities; and b) your local Accessibility Advisory Committee

requirements.label: Inclusive Play Space Features
requirements.description: Incorporate accessibility features, such as sensory and active play components, for children and caregivers with various disabilities into outdoor play space design.

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Outdoor play spaces must have ground surface that is firm, stable, impact-attenuating for injury prevention, and provide sufficient clearance for movement.
requirements.evidence: ensure that outdoor play spaces have ground surface that is ﬁ rm, stable, and has impact-attenuating properties for injury prevention and suĚ  cient clearance

requirements.label: Inclusive Play Space Standards Compliance

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: At least one accessible route must connect the play space boundary, ground-level and elevated play components, with a minimum clear width of 1525 mm and a maximum slope of 1:16.

values.text: clear width 1525 mm min, slope 1:16 max

requirements.evidence: Entry / exit points from an accessible route along the boundary of the play space for users of mobility aids to access play components, where there is a change in level.

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms

requirements.evidence: provide clear turning space for mobility aids of 1675 mm (preferred) or 1500 mm (minimum) diameter on the same level as play components.

requirements.label: Accessible Play Surface Materials
requirements.description: Play spaces must provide accessible surface materials such as poured-in-place rubber, accessible turf, rubber mats and tiles, bonded and engineered wood fibers, or shredded rubber.
requirements.evidence: provide accessible surface materials for play spaces such as poured-in-place rubber, accessible turf, rubber mats and tiles, bonded and engineered wood ﬁ bers or shredded rubber.

requirements.label: Transfer Systems for Play Components
requirements.description: Provide transfer systems (e.g., transfer steps or platforms) to connect elevated or ground-level play components, with a minimum clear floor space of 915 mm wide by 1370 mm long adjacent to all transfer locations.

requirements.description: Provide a high tonal contrast between a play component and its surroundings.
requirements.evidence: provide a high tonal contrast between a play component and its surroundings.

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width

requirements.evidence: Ensure that doorways have a minimum clearance of 1 140 mm.

requirements.evidence: accessible washrooms and stalls, access to paper towels and soap dispensers, lever handles, larger handles on locking devices

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: Provide a turning radius to accommodate wheelchairs or scooters.
requirements.evidence: a turning radius to accommodate wheelchairs or scooters

requirements.label: Bariatric Toilet and Shower Accommodation

requirements.evidence: Avoid enclosing walls; instead, use shower curtains...multiple, reinforced grab bars...removable shower head...Avoid curbs into accessible showers.

requirements.description: Allow a larger turning radius for individuals using wheelchairs; recommended 1,900 mm for wheelchairs or commodes and a caregiver.
requirements.evidence: A recommended 1 900 mm turning radius for wheelchairs or commodes and a caregiver is suggested.

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Safe Path of Travel
requirements.description: Provide a barrier-free safe path of travel, accessible entrances (preferably at zero grade with powered doors).
requirements.evidence: require ease of access to buildings and all facilities and services. This includes a barrier-free safe path of travel, accessible entrances (preferably at zero grade with powered doors)

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Provide visual cues such as a light to alert users of a ringing telephone, doorbell, or alarm for people who are deaf or hard of hearing.
requirements.evidence: relies on visual cues such as a light to alert him/her of a ringing telephone, a doorbell, or an alarm
